content.cn_text: 第四部分。您将听到关于极限运动历史的讲座节选。
content.en_text: Section 4. You will hear a part of lecture about history of extreme sport.

content.cn_text: 首先，您将有时间查看问题31至40。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 第四部分。您将有时间查看问题31至40。
content.en_text: Section 4. You have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 第四部分。您将有时间查看问题31至40。
content.en_text: Section 4. You have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 极限运动是非传统运动和活动，要求参与者将运动能力与显著风险相结合。
content.en_text: Extreme sports are non-traditional sports and activities that require participants to combine athletic scale with pronounced risk.

content.cn_text: 很难确切地确定“极限运动”一词何时开始指代某些现代运动。
content.en_text: It is difficult to determine exactly when the term extreme sports came to refer to certain modern sports.

content.cn_text: 虽然许多极限运动，如深潜和冲浪，通常是人类历史的一部分，但许多极限运动常被称为生活方式运动。
content.en_text: While many extreme sports such as deep diving and surfing have usually been part of human history, so many extreme sports are often called lifestyle sports.

content.cn_text: 它们通常涉及速度、高度、身体投入，极限运动的实践可能涉及一些风险、伤害，如果运动员犯了严重错误甚至可能导致死亡。
content.en_text: The often involved speed, height, physical commitment, the practice of extreme sports can involve some risks, injuries and even death if the athlete made a serious mistake.

content.cn_text: 技术进步带来了更可靠的设备，这使得各种运动成为更安全的活动。
content.en_text: Technology who advances have brought more reliable equipment which making various sports are safer activity.

content.cn_text: 极限运动的全球传播归因于媒体。
content.en_text: The worldwide spread of extreme sports is attributed to the media.

content.cn_text: 电视和电影推动了极限运动的广泛参与。
content.en_text: Television and the movies have been responsible for gaining a widespread participation in extreme sports.

content.cn_text: 在19世纪，劳动力对极限运动的接受度扩大了。
content.en_text: In the 19th century, the acceptance by workforce extended.

content.cn_text: 对于那些休闲偏好较低的男性来说，周日通常无需劳动。
content.en_text: For those men with lower leisure preference, Sundays were generally free from labor.

content.cn_text: 一家公司的工人在空闲时间研究了一项极限运动。
content.en_text: One extreme sport was studied by workers from a company in its free time.

content.cn_text: 雇主们普遍认为这种休闲活动提高了生产力，最重要的是提高了工人对公司的忠诚度。
content.en_text: Employers commonly believe this leisure activity increased the productivity and most importantly increased workers' loyalty to the company.

content.cn_text: 许多人认为19世纪是真正现代游戏出现的时期。
content.en_text: The 19th century is considered by many to be the period when a truly modern game emerged.

content.cn_text: 促成这种现代化的主要变化是游戏设备的改进。
content.en_text: The change that primarily spawned this modernization were to be the equipment of the game.

content.cn_text: 其他运动，如高尔夫，在上层阶级和贵族中流行并经常参与。
content.en_text: Other sports such as golf was popularized and regularly attended by upper classes and nobility.

content.cn_text: 早期的G高尔夫球杆由与现代球杆相似的材料制成：木材和铁。
content.en_text: Early golf clubs were made of materials similar to today's club, wood and iron.

content.cn_text: 然而，旧高尔夫球杆与现代球杆之间最大的区别在于用途。
content.en_text: However, the biggest difference between the old golf clubs and modern ones was the use.

content.cn_text: 在20世纪，你可能会好奇，与许多传统运动如足球或篮球相比，是什么吸引人们参与极限运动？
content.en_text: In the 20th century, you may be wondering, what is it that attracts people's extreme sports in comparison to many traditional sports like football or basketball?

content.cn_text: 如今，几乎所有竞技团队运动的教练、球员和球迷似乎都同意的一点是，这些比赛有太多的规定。
content.en_text: One of the few things that coaches, players and fans of nearly every competitive team sports seem to agree on these days is that there are too many regulations for these games.

content.cn_text: 其次，大多数参与极限运动的人不觉得需要持续的训练和身体素质调整。
content.en_text: Secondly, most of the people involved in extreme sports do not feel the need for constant training and physical conditioning of their body.

content.cn_text: 这导致了热爱极限运动的人们在年老时不再参与那些比赛。
content.en_text: This leads to the stage where people love extreme sports, do not take part in those games as they grow old.

content.cn_text: 最后，参与者描述了极限运动中体验、控制和克服恐惧如何让他们积极地改变日常生活。
content.en_text: Finally, participants described how extreme sports experiencing, controlling and overcome fear made them positively change everyday life.

content.cn_text: 一位登山者将处理恐惧描述为赋能，并在事后感到非常平静。
content.en_text: A mountain climber described the dealing with fear as empowering and feeling the very at peace afterwards.

content.cn_text: 一位定点跳伞者将这项追求描述为跳入生活而非在边缘颤抖的终极比喻。
content.en_text: A base jumper described the pursuit as ultimate metaphor for jumping into the life rather than standing on the edge quivering.

content.cn_text: 在评估纳税人或粉丝参与竞标重大赛事、建造新体育场甚至试图为运动队获取新许可证时，
content.en_text: In assessing the use of taxpayers of fans in, bidding for major event or building a new stadium or even trying to skewer a new license for sports team,

content.cn_text: 年轻的体育参与者明确希望政府将重点放在娱乐性运动上。
content.en_text: young sports participants clearly want government to put their emphasis on entertainment sports.

content.cn_text: 每个人都可以参与，每个人都会玩得开心，每个人都可以参与其中。
content.en_text: Everyone can participate, everyone will have fun and everyone can be involved.

content.cn_text: 个人竞技、限制、要求和其他顾虑根本不属于考虑范围。
content.en_text: Individual competition, limitations, requirements and other concerns simply aren't part of the equation.

content.cn_text: 年轻人处理自我存在，以寻找负责体育的权威机构。
content.en_text: Young people dealt with self-existence to find authorities which are responsible for sport.

content.cn_text: 在体育领域打击种族主义，迫使包括公共权力机构在内的每个人都参与其中，年轻人以其独特的方式应对不断变化的世界，并正在建立他们自己的社区。
content.en_text: Combating racism in sport forced to everyone, including public authorities, young people's responses to the changing world and their unique way is building their own community.

content.cn_text: 极限运动在今天掀起巨大热潮，这种受欢迎背后的心理似乎是人类冒险寻求刺激的天性。
content.en_text: A extreme sports are huge craze today, the psychology behind this popularity seems to be human nature to take risk of thrill.

content.cn_text: 而冒险继续吸引着大众。
content.en_text: And the adventure continues to attract masses.

groups.desc:
Complete the following sentences
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER in each sentence.
groups.title: Question 31-40

items.title: Extreme sport is also known as 31. _______ sports
An extreme sport was created by company 33. _______ in 19th century to boost loyalty.
At the end of the 19th century, other sports had been created like 34. _______ in higher social class.
Why people don’t like 35. _______sports (like team sports. E.g. basketball)
Young people think that government should provide them with more 39. _______ sports instead of individual competition.
Young people tend to think that extreme sports is their attitude to the new world and forming their 40. _______
